title: Melted Savory Korean Rice Stew
description: A salty, velvety one-pot Korean (Gangwon) dish featuring duck fat, lamination-puff, and shojin-friendly ingredients for a comforting meal.
instructions: Heat duck fat in a heavy-bottom pot over medium until completely melted and fragrant.
Add the uncooked rice to the pot and stir until each grain is coated and slightly toasted.
Pour in the water and fish stock, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover to simmer for 10 minutes, or until the rice is partially cooked.
Stir in gochujang, gochugaru, and sesame seed oil, mixing until the sauce is evenly distributed throughout the rice.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms, tofu cubes, and salt, and continue to simmer for another 8 minutes, or until the rice is fully cooked and the stew has a velvety consistency.
Stir in the sliced scallions and sprinkle sesame seeds just before serving to add freshness and crunch.
Keep the stew at low heat for 2 minutes to meld the flavors together.
Ladle the hot stew into bowls and enjoy immediately.
